2604.25525.title: From Chatbots to Confidants: A Cross-Cultural Study of LLM Adoption for Emotional Support
2604.25525.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used not only for instrumental tasks, but as always-available and non-judgmental confidants for emotional support. Yet what drives adoption and how users perceive emotional support interactions across countries remains unknown. To address this gap, we present the first large-scale cross-cultural study of LLM use for emotional support, surveying 4,641 participants across seven countries (USA, UK, Germany, France, Spain, Italy, and The Netherlands). Our results show that adoption rates vary dramatically across countries (from 20% to 59%). Using mixed models that separate cultural effects from demographic composition, we find that: Being aged 25-44, religious, married, and of higher socioeconomic status are predictors of positive perceptions (trust, usage, perceived benefits), with socioeconomic status being the strongest. English-speaking countries consistently show more positive perceptions than Continental European countries. We further collect a corpus of 731 real multilingual prompts from user interactions, showing that users mainly seek help for loneliness, stress, relationship conflicts, and mental health struggles. Our findings reveal that LLM emotional support use is shaped by a complex sociotechnical landscape and call for a broader research agenda examining how these systems can be developed, deployed, and governed to ensure safe and informed access.
2604.25525.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）不僅被用於工具性任務，還被作為隨時可用且不加評判的情感支持者。<lb/>然而，推動採用的因素以及用戶在不同國家對情感支持互動的看法仍然未知。<lb/>為了填補這一空白，我們呈現了首個針對情感支持的LLM使用的大規模跨文化研究，調查了來自七個國家（美國、英國、德國、法國、西班牙、意大利和荷蘭）的4,641名參與者。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，各國的採用率差異顯著（從20%到59%）。<lb/>使用混合模型將文化效應與人口組成分開，我們發現：年齡在25-44歲之間、宗教信仰、已婚以及較高的社會經濟地位是正面看法（信任、使用、感知利益）的預測因子，其中社會經濟地位是最強的預測因子。<lb/>英語國家的正面看法始終高於歐洲大陸國家。<lb/>我們進一步收集了731個來自用戶互動的真實多語言提示的語料，顯示用戶主要尋求對孤獨、壓力、關係衝突和心理健康問題的幫助。<lb/>我們的研究結果揭示了LLM情感支持的使用受到複雜的社會技術環境的影響，並呼籲更廣泛的研究議程，檢視如何開發、部署和管理這些系統，以確保安全和知情的訪問。
2604.25525.author: Natalia Amat-Lefort et.al.
2604.25525.authors: Natalia Amat-Lefort, Mert Yazan, Amanda Cercas Curry, Flor Miriam Plaza-del-Arco
